title: Voice Quality Tanked at Branch 12 After Policy Update

ticket.summary: Since yesterday's SD-WAN policy push, users at Branch 12 report choppy, robotic-sounding calls. Data applications feel normal. Other branches are unaffected. Yesterday afternoon the network team activated an updated centralized policy in vManage intended to steer bulk backup traffic onto the internet transport.

topology.description: Branch 12's Catalyst SD-WAN edge (cedge-br12) has two transports: MPLS (biz-internet class, low latency/jitter) and broadband internet. The intended design steers voice (DSCP EF) over MPLS and bulk/backup traffic over internet, via a centralized data policy applied in vManage. Yesterday's policy revision added a new sequence for backup traffic.

logs.content:
2026-07-16 15:22:04 policy 'DATA-POLICY-BRANCHES' activated by admin j.arnett (rev 14)
2026-07-16 15:22:04 change summary: added sequence 35 'Backup-to-Internet' (match source 10.12.0.0/16 -> set local-tloc color biz-internet)

2026-07-16 15:25 MOS dropped from 4.3 to 2.6 for all Branch 12 calls
2026-07-16 15:25 jitter avg 38ms (baseline 3ms), loss 1.8%

cli_outputs.output:
data-policy DATA-POLICY-BRANCHES
 direction from-service
  sequence 35  Backup-to-Internet
   match
    source-ip 10.12.0.0/16
   action accept
    set
     local-tloc color biz-internet
  sequence 45  Voice-to-MPLS
   match
    dscp 46
   action accept
    set
     local-tloc color mpls

cli_outputs.command: show sdwan app-fwd cflowd flows | include 10.12.150
cli_outputs.output:
src 10.12.150.21 dst 10.0.5.10 dscp 46 out-intf ge0/1 (biz-internet)  <- voice RTP
src 10.12.150.34 dst 10.0.5.10 dscp 46 out-intf ge0/1 (biz-internet)  <- voice RTP

monitoring.notes: Per-transport SLA graphs for Branch 12 show the MPLS tunnel healthy (jitter 2-4ms) and the internet tunnel at 30-45ms jitter with periodic loss — normal for broadband. At 15:25 yesterday, voice flows (DSCP 46) visibly shifted from the MPLS tunnel to the internet tunnel in the flow records, coinciding exactly with the rev-14 policy activation. Branch 12's entire site prefix is 10.12.0.0/16, which includes the voice subnet 10.12.150.0/24.

hints: Data policy sequences are evaluated top-down and stop at the first match — check which sequence Branch 12's voice traffic hits first.
The new sequence 35 matches source 10.12.0.0/16 — does that include the voice subnet, and does it come before or after the voice sequence (45)?

Sequence 35 'Backup-to-Internet' matches all of 10.12.0.0/16 (which contains the voice subnet 10.12.150.0/24) and sits above sequence 45, so voice is steered to biz-internet before the DSCP-46 rule is ever evaluated. Narrow the match to the backup server subnet or move the voice sequence above it.

solution.root_cause: The rev-14 centralized data policy added sequence 35 matching source 10.12.0.0/16 (the entire Branch 12 site, including the voice subnet 10.12.150.0/24) with 'set local-tloc color biz-internet'. Because sequences evaluate top-down and first-match wins, voice traffic now matches sequence 35 before ever reaching the voice sequence 45 (dscp 46 -> mpls), so RTP rides the high-jitter internet transport and call quality collapsed.
solution.fix: Narrow sequence 35's match to the actual backup subnet (e.g. 10.12.200.0/24) or add a dscp 46 exclusion, or reorder so the Voice-to-MPLS sequence precedes the backup sequence; then re-activate the policy and confirm DSCP-46 flows return to the mpls TLOC.
